title: Applied Macroeconomics & Econometrics Center (AMEC)

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This source is a navigation/listing page from the New York Fed's Applied Macroeconomics & Econometrics Center, providing links to various macroeconomic research tools (NY Fed Staff Nowcast, R-Star estimates, Yield Curve indicators, DSGE model forecasts) and a publications archive. The page does not contain specific policy decisions, data releases, or actionable research content—it is an index/portal page. Per Core Rule 6, listing pages and homepages are non-archiveable and not suitable for research note creation.
key_points: Page is a navigation/listing portal for the NY Fed AMEC research center, not an actionable document

Contains links to tools: Global Supply Chain Pressure Index, Labor Market Tightness Index, MCT Inflation, NY Fed DSGE Model Forecast, NY Fed Staff Nowcast, Outlook-at-Risk, R-Star, Yield Curve, Treasury Term Premia
Lists featured academic publications from 2022-2024 on topics including monetary policy, market concentration, exchange rates, and financial stability
No specific FOMC statements, rate decisions, economic data releases, or policy guidance present
The page references some March 2026 content (Staff Nowcast at 2.1% for 2026:Q1, R-Star through 2025:Q4) but these are secondary references within an index, not the primary content
Recent publication archive shows research from 2022-2024 covering topics like wage-price pass-through, monetary spillovers, and credit crises
why_it_matters: This source is not actionable for market structure or macro catalysts research. It is a website index/navigation page that provides no specific data, policy decisions, or analytical content to support investment decisions or risk management.
notes: Non-archivable source: This is a homepage/listing page from the NY Fed AMEC website. The page itself contains no specific policy content, data releases, or market structure insights—it is a navigation portal. The references to recent content (March 2026 Nowcast at 2.1%, R-Star estimates) are incidental mentions within an index, not the primary document content. Source should be flagged as non-archiveable per Core Rule 6 and not ingested into the research archive as a standalone record.

reasoning: Source is a listing/navigation page, not an actionable document
